A.notes: Mentions pairing with Dev and keeping code straightforward, but doesn't identify Dev as junior or proactively assume React/TS. Minimal stored knowledge surfaced.

B.notes: Knows React/TypeScript and references banking platform context. Mentions pairing with Dev but doesn't acknowledge junior status or calibrate complexity. gRPC mention may be relevant stored knowledge or a guess.

F.notes: No mention of Dev, no mention of React/TS, no stored knowledge surfaced at all. Responds as if starting from zero context. Clean but empty.

analysis: System E is the strongest performer, surfacing all key signals — Dev's junior status, React/TS stack, 6 months experience — while also proactively applying the 'no over-engineering' preference and offering a teaching-oriented approach, all woven naturally into the response. System D surfaces equally rich knowledge but reads more like reciting retrieved facts in the opening line. Systems G and H both identify Dev correctly and calibrate appropriately, with H integrating knowledge more naturally but potentially over-reaching with the SNS mention. System C takes a subtler approach, referencing Dev 'ramping up' and Marcus/Linear without explicitly stating stored facts, which feels natural but surfaces less. System B shows some domain knowledge (banking, gRPC) but misses the critical junior calibration. System A barely surfaces stored knowledge beyond knowing Dev exists. System F demonstrates zero retrieval — no mention of Dev, the stack, or any stored context whatsoever.
